contract.name: v2/awards/last_updated.md
contract.description: Returns the most recent awards dataset refresh date as a single MM/DD/YYYY string; GET is the only method that produces the payload.

inputSchema.description: Endpoint does not expect query or body parameters; unexpected query keys are ignored.

last_updated.description: Most recent awards data refresh date formatted as MM/DD/YYYY; docs claim it may be empty when no data exists but that state is unverified.

quirks: Trailing slash is optional; /api/v2/awards/last_updated returns the same payload as the documented path with a slash.
Ignores unexpected query parameters and still emits the same timestamp.
Response body is a display string in MM/DD/YYYY format rather than ISO-8601.
Responses are frequently served from cache (Cache-Trace: hit-cache) and may include cookies even for GET.
risks: Clients must handle the MM/DD/YYYY string carefully to avoid mis-parsing or incorrect ordering because no timezone information is provided.
Cached responses can lag behind the authoritative refresh event; callers who need real-time data may need to bypass caches.
gaps: Docs describe an empty-string sentinel when no award data exists, but that behavior has not been observed.
The timezone or schedule that determines the reported date is undocumented.

probes.notes: Baseline documented path returns the MM/DD/YYYY string as described.
Trailing slash is optional; both URIs return the same payload.
Extraneous query parameters are ignored; response matches the baseline.
Invalid methods yield a JSON 405 explaining the rejection.

gaps: Docs describe an empty-string sentinel when no award data exists, but that behavior has not been observed.
The timezone or schedule that determines the reported date is undocumented.
risks: Clients must handle the MM/DD/YYYY string carefully to avoid mis-parsing or incorrect ordering because no timezone information is provided.
Cached responses can lag behind the authoritative refresh event; callers who need real-time data may need to bypass caches.
